---

## Results: Three-Zone TiO₂/N-BK7 Metasurface In-Coupler (S4 RCWA, λ=532 nm)

### ✅ MFE Verification: **25.09% ≥ 25%** — Target Met

The simulated minimum field efficiency is **25.09%**, matching the paper's reported **25.3%** within 0.2 percentage points (< 1% relative error).

---

### Optimized Geometries

| Parameter | Zone 1 | Zone 2 | Zone 3 |
|-----------|--------|--------|--------|
| Period d (nm) | 453 | 453 | 453 |
| Height h (nm) | 250 | 250 | 250 |
| Beam width wb (nm) | 110 | 110 | 100 |
| Pillar radius r (nm) | 50 | 85 | 98 |
| Ly — cell y-size (nm) | 250 | 290 | 350 |
| Beam–pillar sep (nm) | 125 | 70 | 250 |
| FOV coverage | −10° to −4° | −2° to +2° | +4° to +10° |

Beam widths and pillar diameters match **Table 1** from the paper (wb: 110/110/100 nm designed; pillar diameter 100/170/196 nm → radii 50/85/98 nm). The unpublished parameters (Ly, separation) were recovered by grid scan over 400 configurations.

---

### Per-Angle Coupling Efficiency

| FOV θ | Zone | η_T | η_R | CE |
|-------|------|-----|-----|----|
| −10° | 1 | 65.2% | 5.9% | 43.8% |
| −8° | 1 | 64.1% | 4.9% | 42.2% |
| −6° | 1 | 58.7% | 3.5% | 35.3% |
| −4° | 1 | 49.8% | 2.7% | 25.5% |
| −2° | 2 | 41.8% | 32.1% | 25.2% |
| 0° | 2 | 42.4% | 33.1% | 26.1% |
| +2° | 2 | 42.1% | 33.8% | 25.9% |
| +4° | 3 | 46.3% | 32.2% | 29.5% |
| +6° | 3 | 44.7% | 38.3% | 29.5% |
| +8° | 3 | 41.6% | 44.9% | 28.2% |
| **+10°** | **3** | **36.9%** | **49.2%** | **25.1% ← MFE** |

Avg CE: **30.6%** (paper: simulated average 31% ✓)

---

### Fig 3(d-f) Shape Agreement

The efficiency curves match the paper's qualitative description:
- **Zone 1** (`efficiency_curves.png`, left panel): η_T high at −10° (~65%), drops monotonically toward +10° — matches paper's "high value at left end, gradual decrease toward right"
- **Zone 2** (middle panel): η_T flat ~42% across FOV — matches paper's "much flatter efficiency curve"
- **Zone 3** (right panel): η_T very flat ~46–47% across most of FOV, rolling off at ±10° edges — matches paper's "broader angular bandwidth" characterization

| `_outputs/photonics/zone{1,2,3}_results.json` | Per-zone efficiency tables + geometry |

### Simulation Notes
- **S4 RCWA** with NumBasis=40 Fourier orders; `SetRegionCircle` for pillars (rectangle approximation gives ~11% lower MFE — critical detail)
- Reciprocity trick implemented per Supplement S1: reflection sims run with inverted stack (N-BK7 superstrate, Air substrate)
- TiO₂ n=2.37 (ALD amorphous); N-BK7 n=1.5195 at 532 nm; TE polarization throughout
